authoritative_sources.title: Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic Certification Criteria
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA is the primary federal behavioral-health agency. The 2023 CCBHC criteria are authoritative public operating guidance for behavioral-health clinic screening, assessment, person-centered treatment planning, care coordination, crisis continuity, quality reporting, and health IT expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Program-model grounding, intake readiness, treatment-plan, and coordination evidence control
used_for: care coordination, ADT, and health IT record expectations

authoritative_sources.title: State Operations Manual Appendix F: Guidance to Surveyors, Community Mental Health Centers
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is authoritative for Medicare-participating CMHCs. Appendix F is the public operating baseline for comprehensive assessment, interdisciplinary treatment-team coordination, active treatment-plan documentation, progress-note expectations, discharge or transfer summaries, and QAPI-sensitive recordkeeping in community mental health operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Comprehensive-assessment validation, active treatment-plan control, and transfer or discharge evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 National Guidelines for a Behavioral Health Coordinated System of Crisis Care
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's national crisis-care guideline is a recognized public workflow reference for crisis-line, mobile-crisis, stabilization, follow-up, transfer, and handoff expectations across the behavioral-health system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Crisis transition, follow-up tracking, and community handoff routing

authoritative_sources.title: Quality Measures for Behavioral Health Clinics: Technical Specifications and Resource Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SAMHSA's 2024 behavioral-health clinic quality-measures manual is a recognized workflow reference for the evidence fields, data sources, measure windows, and follow-up reporting terminology that operations teams track for behavioral-health service completion and transition workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measure-window tracking, follow-up evidence handling, and reporting-source alignment
used_for: follow-up after emergency or inpatient discharge terminology
record-source definitions for reporting and closure evidence
exception handling when quality-reporting evidence and clinical records diverge

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Privacy Rule and Sharing Information Related to Mental Health
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is authoritative for HIPAA Privacy Rule interpretation. This guidance is the public control baseline for mental-health information sharing, family involvement, professional-judgment limits, and serious-and-imminent-threat disclosure boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Disclosure review, family or community communication handling, and privacy escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fact Sheet 42 CFR Part 2 Final Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS is authoritative for the current Part 2 baseline. The fact sheet is the public control reference when behavioral-health records include SUD treatment information that carries stricter consent, redisclosure, and breach-handling requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Controlled disclosure handling for co-mingled behavioral-health and SUD records
used_for: privacy-related escalation triggers when SUD records are present

workflow_stages.name: Program-model and intake grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the tenant's behavioral-health service model, jurisdiction, referral posture, payer or funding context, current episode stage, and required consent or intake baseline before work is accepted as in scope.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Assessment and treatment-plan readiness verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify assessment completion, person-centered treatment-plan status, assigned clinician or care coordinator, required authorizations, and whether the case can advance into active operational administration.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Scheduling, care coordination, and crisis-transition administration
workflow_stages.description: Track appointments, no-show outreach, crisis or hospital follow-up obligations, care-coordination handoffs, and reporting-window evidence without making licensed clinical decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, privacy, and authorization handling
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing documents, stale authorizations, disclosure requests, incident or grievance workflow, conflicting records, and quality-reporting gaps by preserving evidence and routing to the right owner rather than improvising.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Transfer, discharge, and closure evidence control
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when discharge or transition records, follow-up evidence, referral handoffs, required completion artifacts, and privacy-sensitive controls are reconciled across the governing systems of record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: referrals, intake packets, and program-admission documentation
signed consent, release-of-information records, and communication restrictions
comprehensive or biopsychosocial assessments and person-centered treatment plans
appointment schedules, no-show logs, outreach attempts, and care-coordination notes
crisis or safety plan references, ADT notifications, emergency or inpatient discharge notices, and mobile-crisis handoff records
authorization submissions, payer correspondence, and utilization-review records
incident reports, grievance logs, privacy-escalation records, and QAPI-sensitive evidence trails
transfer paperwork, discharge summaries, community referral handoffs, completion records, and quality-measure reporting extracts
systems_of_record: behavioral-health EHR or patient record system
hospital-notification or ADT feed and transition interface
crisis call-center, mobile-crisis, or stabilization workflow when applicable
incident, grievance, or compliance case-management system
quality-measure reporting registry or data mart

allowed: Classify behavioral-health operations work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, rework, transfer, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete operational workflow steps based on documented referral, consent, assessment, treatment-plan, scheduling, crisis-transition, authorization, and discharge evidence.
Prepare evidence-backed status summaries, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages without overstating authority.
escalate: Required service-model, jurisdiction, assessment, authorization, crisis-transition, consent, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires diagnosis, risk judgment, involuntary-treatment evaluation, medication authority, privacy interpretation, legal advice, safety intervention, or regulator or payer signoff.
A state rule, county contract, payer requirement, or accreditor expectation materially changes the workflow.
A privacy, crisis, safety, or SUD redisclosure concern changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Mark work complete without the required referral, consent, assessment, treatment-plan, follow-up, and discharge evidence when applicable.
Disclose behavioral-health records, psychotherapy scheduling information, or SUD-related notes without a clear permitted-use basis and authorized owner.
Treat behavioral-health operations like generic scheduling or CRM administration by ignoring treatment-plan, crisis-transition, consent, authorization, or discharge controls.

domain_failure_modes: a referral or transition is advanced even though consent, assessment, treatment-plan, or discharge evidence is missing or stale
a family, school, court, or community request triggers disclosure of mental-health or SUD-related records without a valid permission or approved legal basis
crisis, emergency, or inpatient follow-up is handled like routine scheduling and fails to preserve crisis-transition records, handoff evidence, or measure windows
licensed clinical decisions about level of care, involuntary treatment, risk, or crisis disposition are made by operations because the lane boundary is underspecified
EHR, scheduling, ADT, payer, and care-management systems disagree on current case state and the work is still marked complete
tenant or jurisdiction-specific behavioral-health workflow differences are guessed instead of retrieved and escalated
industry-specific behavioral-health terminology is replaced by generic service-desk language that omits treatment plans, crisis follow-up, consent, and discharge artifacts

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Behavioral-health providers operate under materially different service models such as CCBHC, CMHC, crisis-only, ACT, school-based, county-contracted, residential, or peer-support programs, and the governing state or county workflow may change closure, handoff, and documentation rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: This pack uses SAMHSA, CMS, and HHS materials as the public baseline. Runtime must retrieve the actual program model, jurisdiction, and tenant authority map before applying state-specific or contract-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific EHR, ADT, crisis-vendor, payer, and document-repository configurations can materially change which system is the operational source of truth and whether SUD-protected information is co-mingled with general behavioral-health records.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane remains validated because these are explicit tenant prerequisites, not gaps in the public baseline. The specialist escalates when source-of-truth or privacy posture cannot be established.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, privacy, and failure behavior instead of generic healthcare operations scripting.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because service-model-specific clinical and legal interpretation is explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at behavioral-health operations administration. Diagnosis, risk determination, crisis disposition, medication authority, privacy interpretation, and formal quality or regulatory signoff remain outside the lane.
